title: LumberChunker: Dynamic Text Segmentation for Dense Retrieval and RAG

defines.name: LumberChunker
defines.definition: An LLM-driven dynamic text segmentation method that iteratively identifies content shift points to create semantically independent chunks of variable size.

defines.name: GutenQA
defines.definition: A benchmark of 3,000 needle-in-a-haystack question-answer pairs derived from 100 public domain narrative books from Project Gutenberg for evaluating retrieval and RAG chunking methods.

problem: Fixed-size or simple chunking strategies can hurt dense retrieval quality and RAG performance because they fail to capture semantically independent content boundaries.
